assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions job termination and slice effects but doesn't specifically explain that TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step rather than through rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response states 'All 4 slices affected - Maintenance typically affects entire zones/regions where your slices are located' which addresses this expectation

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response clearly states 'Complete job termination - Your training will stop abruptly when nodes are drained' which covers simultaneous pod restart and job interruption

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'Worst case: 4-8 hours (if slice availability issues)' which addresses the potential for extended downtime due to resource availability

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'Save an extra checkpoint manually before the window' in the pre-maintenance actions section

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes checkpoint strategy configuration and recovery automation scripts that address automatic resume functionality

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the option to defer maintenance events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions maintenance windows in the context of timing, it doesn't suggest using maintenance windows to schedule events during planned training breaks
